name: fastjsonschema (Fast JSON Schema Validator)

performance.notes: 2-4x faster than jsonschema via compiled Python validators

agent_readiness.notes: Fast JSON schema validation via compiled validators. import fastjsonschema. schema = {'type': 'object', 'properties': {'name': {'type': 'string'}, 'age': {'type': 'integer', 'minimum': 0}}, 'required': ['name']}. validate = fastjsonschema.compile(schema). validate({'name': 'John', 'age': 30}) → passes. validate({'age': 'bad'}) → raises fastjsonschema.JsonSchemaValueException. compile() generates optimized Python code at compile time. Supports JSON Schema draft 4, 6, 7. $ref support. format validators. Custom format validators: fastjsonschema.FormatError. Used internally by Jupyter for notebook format validation.

gotchas: Supports up to JSON Schema draft 7 — not draft 2019-09 or 2020-12
Error messages less descriptive than jsonschema library
compile() step required upfront — not suitable for dynamic schemas
Less feature-complete than jsonschema for edge cases
best_when: High-throughput JSON validation where performance matters
Validating known schemas at application startup (compile once, use many)
When jsonschema is the bottleneck in hot paths
avoid_when: Dynamic or unknown schemas at runtime
When full jsonschema feature parity is needed (use jsonschema)
summary: fastjsonschema validates JSON 2-4x faster than jsonschema via compiled validators. compile(schema) → validate fn; validate(data) raises JsonSchemaValueException. JSON Schema draft 4/6/7. $ref support. BSD-3-Clause. Used by Jupyter internally. Best for high-throughput validation of known schemas. No draft 2019-09+. Error messages less helpful than jsonschema. Compile once, validate many.
